calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-29-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Vivian Holley for (administrator) Reverend Dairy G. Johnson, Sr.

Ms. Holley reported that out of the 31 heirs, 11 have refused to sign receipts, that the sister has filed a declaration stating that they have received the money and at this date all receipts possible have been filed. Ms. Holley reported that there was an irregularity with notice, that she had submitted filings to a service who did not actually execute the filing until the next day but that it still gave 34 days notice, has cleared up the misspelled name on the order (Wright) and that there are still no clear indication as to how to make the distribution to Shawntanee and Kirk. In regards to her extraordinary fees, Ms. Holley stated that there were many issues with the sale of the property, that the administrator could not be bonded forcing her to put the proceeds into an account with UBS which is like a blocked account and she spent much time dealing with one of the heirs who is homeless. A revised order was presented to the court and the accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy McConoco. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-23-2014 Continued To Sep-29-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Vivian Holley for Dairy Johnson Sr.

Ms. Holley provided family history, addressed the issues raised in the Probate Examiner's notes and presented oral argument to support her position with regard to distribution to Shawntanee and Kirk, and extraordinary fees. The accounting petition was taken under submission with the understanding that the matter may be continued. Ms. Holley also advised the Court that Sandra Wright Trustee of the Texas Estate was found and is alive.

Judge: John Dearman. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)
